evidence: Ivor's framing: 'The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.'

V's framing: 'Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.'

V's framing: 'Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.'
Synthesis: 'In a world of AI-generated everything, the only reliable signal is direct interaction over time' - validates V's cornerstone on trust latency
Ivor offered to make Handshake intro via his Strategic Ops hire: 'she could be probably a good connect to Handshake'
V: 'Handshake is actually the actual perfect acquirer... they're very present in the career center'

source_display: The Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Strategic insight: 'Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state. The gap between stated job requirements and actual selection preferences is a structural feature of hiring markets, not a bug.'

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: V: 'What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters.' - Strategic pivot decision

source_display: Response Rate as the Only Metric That Matters
evidence: Logan's insight: 'use response rates as the convincing proxy — our users pay attention to our recommendations'

source_display: Information Efficiency Has Inverted Recruiter Value
evidence: Indian recruiter anecdote: 'I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.' - Evidence that information commoditization already happened before AI

source_display: Response Rate as the Only Metric That Matters
evidence: Frames Careerspan as candidate-centric even when selling to employers: 'The business model could be structured around response rate SLAs'
Ilse's prior proposal affirmed: 'Applicants are not auto-submitted to roles. They must explicitly click submit' - protects candidate agency and validates response rate metrics

source_display: Sora Auditorium Video as Product Vision
evidence: Ilse's recollection of generated Sora video: auditorium full of candidates empties to three people after clicking Careerspan button - 'hiring is too loud, and we make it quiet'
V on Superposition: 'They wouldn't bring users. They might not even bring significant revenue. But their logo creates a trust cascade. This is pure signaling economics—the brand IS the product.'
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users' was essentially: 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' Inverts network effects playbook to exclusivity positioning.
Logan's biotech friend tangent: disrupted industry by going directly to morgues instead of middlemen. Applicable provocation: 'Who is Careerspan's middleman that could be bypassed?'
V pursuing dual-track strategy: brand names (Superposition) for credibility, individual recruiters for execution
Ivor's validation of Handshake as acquisition target aligns with V's ongoing acquihire thesis - 'they're very present in the career center' maps to team-outcome strategy

V's pivot explanation: 'realized they've built a high-signal community, ~3-4K users, 1/3+ with multiple stories, deep data unlike any other platform, all from curated communities' - validates taste curation thesis
